strengths: The mutation was highly successful, increasing the trade count from 27 in the parent spec to 2,249, providing a statistically robust sample for evaluation.
The spec demonstrates an exceptionally strong predictive edge with an aggregate Win Rate of 96.2% over a large number of trades.
Achieved a high aggregate expectancy of +6.83 bps per trade, confirming significant profitability.
The signal is very consistent on symbol 000660, with a per-session WR above 97% on all three days, reaching 100% on 20260317.
weaknesses: Performance on symbol 005930 is less consistent, with per-session Win Rates ranging from 89.0% to 97.3%, suggesting the signal's effectiveness is regime-dependent.
The horizon curve indicates the signal's predictive power decays at longer horizons, with the aggregate Win Rate dropping from 96.2% at 10 ticks to 67.6% at 200 ticks.
recommended_direction_reasoning: With an exceptional WR of 96.2% over 2,249 trades, simple threshold tightening offers diminishing returns. The observed variance in performance across sessions (e.g., 005930 WR ranging from 89.0% to 97.3%) suggests that a fixed threshold is suboptimal. Introducing a time-varying or adaptive threshold, such as one based on the rolling standard deviation of the signal, is the logical next step to handle different market regimes and potentially improve consistency.
